# wordpress Provider

Provider for managing WordPress resources.

Provider settings can also be read from environment variables: `WP_TF_PROVIDER_HOST`, `WP_TF_PROVIDER_USERNAME`, and `WP_TF_PROVIDER_PASSWORD`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "wordpress" {
  host     = "http://localhost:8888/wp-json/wp/v2"
  username = "username"
  password = "application_password"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `host` (String) The base URL of the WordPress site, including the REST API endpoint. Example: `http://localhost:8888/wp-json/wp/v2`. Can also be set via the `WP_TF_PROVIDER_HOST` or `WORDPRESS_HOST` environment variables.
- `password` (String) Password to authenticate with. Needs to be an application password, otherwise you will encounter `401 Unauthorized` errors. See https://make.wordpress.org/core/2020/11/05/application-passwords-integration-guide/ for more information. Can also be set via the `WP_TF_PROVIDER_PASSWORD` or `WORDPRESS_PASSWORD` environment variables.
- `username` (String) Username to authenticate with. Example: `admin`. Can also be set via the `WP_TF_PROVIDER_USERNAME` or `WORDPRESS_USERNAME` environment variables.
